article_title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4 With Native Computer Use and Financial Plugins as It Fights to Regain User Trust

content_preview.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4 With Native Computer Use and Financial Plugins as It Fights to Regain User Trust
content_preview.summary: OpenAI releases its most capable model yet with autonomous computer operation and enterprise integrations, but the launch is overshadowed by Pentagon deal fallout and a reported 1.5 million user exodus.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI released GPT-5.4 on March 5, 2026, billing it as its [most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/05/openai-launches-gpt-5-4-with-pro-and-thinking-versions/). The model arrives in three variants -- standard GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4 Thinking for extended reasoning, and GPT-5.4 Pro for maximum performance -- and introduces native computer-use capabilities alongside new enterprise integrations for financial workflows. The launch comes ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 654 words. Clear sections covering capabilities, computer use, enterprise integrations, safety measures, availability, unknowns, and analysis. Appropriate technical depth throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: TechCrunch (verified — confirms March 5 release, three variants, 'most capable' claim, 1M token context, Pro tier availability). Fortune (verified — confirms GPT-5.3 Codex coding lineage, computer use, Excel/Sheets beta, FactSet/MSCI/Third Bridge/Moody's plugins, 33%/18% error reduction, Anthropic competition). OpenAI System Card at deploymentsafety.openai.com (verified — NEW URL replacing dead helpnetsecurity.com from Round 1 — confirms High cybersecurity classification, two-tiered monitoring architecture, Trusted Access for Cyber program, CoT controllability evaluation, 0.3% controllability on 10k-char traces). Gizmodo (verified — confirms 1.5M user loss, Pentagon controversy, Altman 'really painful' quote, APEX-Agents leaderboard claim, employee dissent).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims cross-verified against sources. The 33%/18% error reduction figures confirmed by both Fortune and Gizmodo. Safety Measures section now properly sourced from the authoritative OpenAI system card with specific metrics (0.3% controllability, two-tiered architecture, TAC program). Article appropriately hedges unverified claims ('OpenAI claims', 'has not been independently verified').
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The article reports the Pentagon controversy and user losses factually without editorializing. The Analysis section provides balanced context without taking sides.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GPT-5.4 coverage in published articles.
editor_notes.round_2_note: Re-review after REQUEST_CHANGES. The bot replaced the dead helpnetsecurity.com URL (HTTP 404) with OpenAI's official GPT-5.4 Thinking System Card (deploymentsafety.openai.com/gpt-5-4-thinking). Fetched and verified — it is the authoritative primary source and confirms all safety claims. The Safety Measures subsection was rewritten with more specific details from the system card. The blocking finding from Round 1 is resolved.
editor_notes.allowlist_note: deploymentsafety.openai.com is not in the source allowlist but is an official OpenAI domain — a primary source for safety documentation. This is acceptable and should be added to the allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. The Round 1 dead-link issue has been properly resolved with an authoritative primary source. All four sources now verified and accessible. Ready for publication.
